concepts.title: Atmosphere Composition
concepts.description: The atmosphere is composed of 78% Nitrogen (N2), 21% Oxygen (O2), and 1% other gases like Argon, Carbon Dioxide, and Neon.

concepts.title: Layers of the Atmosphere
concepts.description: The atmosphere is divided into five layers: Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere, and Exosphere.

concepts.title: Thermosphere Characteristics
concepts.description: The Thermosphere extends from 85-600 km above the Earth's surface and is characterized by increasing temperature with altitude and high ionization due to solar radiation.

concepts.title: Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere Mnemonic
concepts.description: Troposphere: Temperature decreases with altitude (Tropo = Top, Temperature drops as you go 'top' up) Stratosphere: Temperature increases with altitude (Strato = Stable, Temperature is stable) Mesosphere: Temperature decreases with altitude again (Mesos = Middle, Temperature drops in the middle)

concepts.title: Atmospheric Pressure Decrease
concepts.description: Students often mistake that atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude, but it actually remains constant at a given level due to the balance between the weight of the air above and the pressure exerted by the air at that level.

concepts.title: Atmospheric Layers
concepts.description: Troposphere: 0-12 km, Stratosphere: 12-50 km, Mesosphere: 50-80 km, Thermosphere: 80-600 km, Exosphere: 600-10,000 km

concepts.title: Ozone Layer
concepts.description: The ozone layer, present in the Stratosphere, protects the Earth from harmful UV radiation, but its depletion due to human activities has raised concerns about its preservation.

concepts.title: Atmospheric Functions
concepts.description: The atmosphere performs several functions, including regulating temperature, providing oxygen, shielding the Earth from harmful radiation, and influencing weather patterns.
